claim: Stay on React 19.2 and turn on React Compiler 1.0 for new code — let it handle memoization; keep existing useMemo/useCallback as escape hatches (don't strip them — removing can change the compiler's o
Target the latest stable RN (0.86); the New Architecture is non-optional from 0.82, so plan any migration around that line.
Run strict TypeScript (strict:true, the TS 6 default) and export a small, deliberate public type surface from libraries.
Split the problem: TanStack Query for server state + Zustand for client/UI state. This is the common, low-regret 2026 pairing.
TanStack Query for REST/server-state caching — the default for client-side data fetching.
React Native → Expo Router if you're on Expo, else React Navigation. Web → use the meta-framework's router (see RB-E-META-FRAMEWORKS), or TanStack Router for a type-safe SPA.
Web: Next.js is the safe default; choose TanStack Start when you want a less-opinionated, Vite-native RSC stack. RN/universal: Expo Router.
React Hook Form + Zod — the common, performant, RN-compatible default.

Share a platform-agnostic logic/hooks package first (the highest-leverage, lowest-risk win). For shared UI on NEW projects, evaluate React Strict DOM or Expo Universal Components — not a fresh react-n
Lightweight desktop → Tauri 2 (native webview, tiny builds). Need maximum ecosystem / deep Node + native integration, or already on it → Electron. Just wrapping an existing web URL → Pake.

Holepunch is a SERVERLESS, local-first alternative to the client-server + REST/GraphQL model: data is a local append-only log (Hypercore), merged across writers with Autobase, indexed in Hyperbee, syn
Web: Tailwind (pair with shadcn/ui — RB-E-COMPONENT-LIBS). React Native: NativeWind if you want Tailwind, else StyleSheet/Unistyles. Prefer zero-runtime/compiler approaches on both.
Own-the-code: shadcn/ui (on Radix/Base UI) + Tailwind — the default for product teams that want control.

React Native → Reanimated 4 + Gesture Handler 3 for real UI-thread animation & gestures (core Animated only for trivial cases). Web (React DOM) → Motion (framer-motion's successor) for declarative lay
RN: FlashList for large lists, FlatList otherwise, ScrollView only for <20 static items. Web: react-virtual / react-window.
Use semantic roles + labels, manage focus order, and announce dynamic updates; test with role-based queries, not data-testid.
Next.js app → next-intl. Otherwise i18next (web + RN, huge ecosystem) or Lingui (compile-time, low boilerplate).
RN: Victory Native (XL) for standard charts; react-native-skia when you need custom/realtime rendering. Web: Recharts (batteries-included) or visx (low-level D3).

Most React apps → TipTap (ProseMirror power with React ergonomics); large-scale / custom-node-heavy → Lexical; maximum control → ProseMirror directly; deeply custom React schemas → Slate/Plate; Notion

React Native → react-native-svg is the default for declarative vector graphics; import static .svg assets via react-native-svg-transformer, and pull icons from an icon set (lucide-react-native, or per
Target the New Architecture (mandatory ≥ RN 0.82). Reach for Expo Modules for most native needs; use Nitro Modules for performance-critical custom native.

Camera → VisionCamera (frame processors) or expo-camera (managed); video calling → LiveKit (open-source) or a hosted SDK (Daily/Agora/Stream); real-time filters → process VisionCamera frames + GPU-com
Small key-value → react-native-mmkv (fast, synchronous). Relational/large → op-sqlite or expo-sqlite. Secrets/tokens → Keychain / secure-store, never AsyncStorage.

On Expo → expo-widgets for iOS widgets/Live Activities (stable in SDK 56, no native code). Bare RN → Voltra 2 (now bare-RN capable). Custom native targets / App Clips / share extensions → Expo Targets
Core KeyboardAvoidingView is fine for simple iOS-first layouts; for cross-platform parity (especially Android + edge-to-edge) use react-native-keyboard-controller — KeyboardAwareScrollView for forms,
Subscriptions → RevenueCat (it absorbs receipt validation + entitlement infra). Direct store billing without a third party → expo-iap (Expo) or react-native-iap.
A scale-stage concern: embed RN into a native app with react-native-brownfield (+ ReactNativeFactory on iOS). For independently-deployable screens / super-apps, look at Granite or Rock.
2D / interactive visuals → react-native-skia. Real game engine → embed Godot (react-native-godot). RN suits supplemental/interactive experiences more than AAA games.
Stay on React Native unless a specific driver pushes you off it — its decade of libraries is a major advantage; Lynx and Valdi are early.
Web: Vite 8 (Rolldown). React Native: Metro (+ Hermes V1, precompiled iOS binaries). Monorepo: pnpm workspaces + Turborepo. Expo for managed velocity.
Unit/component: Vitest (web) or Jest (RN) + React Testing Library. E2E: Playwright (web), Maestro (RN). MSW for API mocks; Storybook for component dev.

Gate tests + typecheck + lint on every PR in CI, and enforce the same locally with a pre-commit hook. For lint/format, default to Biome (one fast tool) unless you need ESLint's plugin ecosystem (react
Sentry for crash + performance monitoring (v8 catches pre-bootstrap crashes); add error boundaries and wire it up at the production stage.

Secrets in Keychain/secure-store (never AsyncStorage); harden the supply chain (block install scripts, gate install-age); device trust via server-side attestation; route per-diff secret/injection chec

Cross-platform on-device models → react-native-executorch; Apple-only → @react-native-ai/apple; local RAG → react-native-rag. Fast-moving (confidence: low) — validate on-device perf before committing.

Vercel AI SDK for streaming chat + tool-calling; assistant-ui/Tambo for prebuilt chat UI; Streamdown to render streaming markdown. Very fast-moving (confidence: low) — treat picks as current-best, not

On Expo/EAS → EAS Update (channels + phased rollouts + rollback, and diffing makes updates dramatically smaller since SDK 55/56). Hard self-host requirement → hot-updater, or the open expo-updates pro

Use fetch — on Expo SDK 56+ you are already on expo/fetch globally. The bigger win in most apps is the CACHE layer above it (TanStack Query, RB-E-DATA), not the client. Reach for react-native-nitro-fe
